KW-01.claim: Source Gate treats confirmed primary material as the evidence basis.
Metadata alone cannot support speaker logic.
Transcript evidence should be preserved before report writing.
Claims remain tied to their source.

KW-02.claim: The SRT contains three timestamped subtitle segments.
The subtitle text states that Source Gate needs confirmed primary material.
Metadata is insufficient to support speaker logic.
The report should preserve transcript evidence and avoid unsupported speaker attribution.

KW-03.claim: The WebVTT has three timestamped cues.
The source defines Source Gate through confirmed primary material.
Metadata alone cannot support speaker logic.
The source requires preserving transcript evidence.

KW-04.claim: The supplied ASR sidecar has two timestamped records.
The ASR text says it preserves evidence.
The transcript remains tied to source spans.
The report must not add observations about media that were not supplied.

KW-05.claim: The local media task is analyzed from the supplied ASR sidecar.
There are two ASR segments covering 0–3 and 3–6 seconds.
Transcript text remains tied to source spans.
No visual or audio fact beyond the supplied transcript is asserted.

KW-06.claim: The project is a source-gated knowledge workflow positioned after Agent-Reach.
It separates acquisition, source gating/evidence, analysis, and report composition.
Search summaries are not promoted to primary sources.
Formal writing separates Source, Inference, and Extension.

KW-07.claim: The user-facing skills divide routing, acquisition, evidence gating/audit, and composition responsibilities.
Only source-confirmed or source-partial material can enter normal or partial analysis.
Metadata, snippets, secondary material, failed material, and unsupported routes cannot unlock a normal final report.
A social post is not a substitute for an embedded video's transcript.

KW-08.claim: The architecture is run-scoped and source-gated from acquisition to report.
Backend capability and evidence target compatibility are independent gates.
A target-compatible primary scope is required before normal analysis.

KW-09.claim: Agent-Reach handles upstream platform selection and acquisition; Knowledge Workflow handles evidence and report readiness.
There are structured-adapter and native-export/import integration paths.
Cookies, tokens, headers, screenshots, page shells, and search-only results are not primary material.
A web_article requires an article/profile body, not a result card.

KW-10.claim: Reports distinguish Source claims, Inference, and Extension.
Reusable claims require accepted and visible claim IDs.
A report requires source status, primary material, an approved quality gate, required sections, and named limits.
Failure output must say what failed, the failure class, and the next user action.
